title: QtLockedFile on container
body:
### Issue Description

Hi, all!

Recently, i had to move from Docker to Podman. And then, I tried to launch QBitTorrent, but...

This is my command;

```bash
podman run -d `
  --log-level=debug `
  --name=qBitTorrent `
  -e PUID=1000 `
  -e PGID=1000 `
  -e WEBUI_PORT=8080 `
  -p 8080:8080 `
  -p 6881:6881 `
  -p 6881:6881/udp `
  -v ./config:/config:z `
  -v ./torrents:/torrents:z `
  -v ./downloads:/downloads:z `
  lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest 
```

The container starts but does not work. Logs:
[terminal]
```
time="2025-04-11T22:32:55+02:00" level=info msg="C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"

QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened
...
```

I know you don't support Podman, but this seems to be a problem related with: "QT" and a file locked somewhere.

Can You please give it a try?

Thanks in advance. ;^)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Download and install Podman for windows
2. Launch the command above.
3. Look at the logs.


### Describe the results you received

The same message one and over again:
"QtLockedFile::lock(): file is not opened"


### Describe the results you expected

http://localhost:8080 working.
But the browser says no data is received.

### Additional information

comments.body: Not being particularly familiar with qbittorrent, there isn't much we can do here. If you can find more about the error, particularly what file it is complaining about being locked, we can at least identify if this is a Podman problem.
Ok, @mheon; I'll try to take a discussion w/ the guys @ linuxserver.io
This doesn't seem like a Windows specific issue, since the error seems to be Linux based. I would recommend removing the 'windows' label from this.
The label is auto-applied if the attached `podman info` from the user indicates they filed the bug using a Windows system; we can remove after the fact if it does not appear to be a Windows bug.

Well so far no information has been provided that this is not a windows specific bug so I think the label should stay on.

The command is being run on windows and it seems to be mounting windows directories, then QT complains about `file is not opened` which could means there is an FS error if it cannot deal with lockfiles maybe?
Anyway if the application is unable to report useful errors then it is not really our problem of figuring out the root cause.

And running the provided command works just fine on my linux box so that would strongly suggest a problem with WSL maybe and IMO all WSL problems should be marked windows.

Fair enough! Could you run WSL logs? We could check for any WSL errors in there. 

Instructions to run WSL logs are here: https://aka.ms/wsllogs and please attach them to this thread and I can go through them.

@PIPOGit One other thing you can try is to to remove the -v options so the application wouldn't use the shared windows file system.

Just thinking about it WSL uses 9p, right? Does 9p even support lock files, i.e. via flock() or fcntl() F_SETLK?

Well... Hi all! And thanks for the support!

I'll try yo explain things. ;)

# The context

## Script Execution, for logs
I downloaded the script from: https://github.com/Microsoft/WSL/blob/master/diagnostics/collect-wsl-logs.ps1
But, unfortunatelly, my Windows OS does NOT allow me to execute it because "unsigned scripts"... You know. So, bad try! ;)

## Original info
The command is basically copied from [here](https://docs.linuxserver.io/images/docker-qbittorrent/#docker-cli-click-here-for-more-info)

## Windows ("*host*") folder
Where I run PODMAN "from":

```
%> gci
Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name
----                 -------------         ------ ----
d-----        06/04/2025      9:23                config
d-----        30/03/2025     12:02                downloads
d-----        24/05/2024     22:55                torrents

```

# First try: Without volumes
## Script
```
Clear-Host
podman container stop qBitTorrent
podman container rm qBitTorrent
podman run -d `
  --log-level=trace `
  --name=qBitTorrent `
  -e PUID=1000 `
  -e PGID=1000 `
  -e WEBUI_PORT=8080 `
  -e TORRENTING_PORT=6881 `
  -p 8080:8080 `
  -p 6881:6881 `
  -p 6881:6881/udp `
  lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest
```

```
Clear-Host
podman container stop qBitTorrent
podman container rm qBitTorrent
podman run -d `
  --log-level=trace `
  --name=qBitTorrent `
  -e PUID=1000 `
  -e PGID=1000 `
  -e WEBUI_PORT=8080 `
  -e TORRENTING_PORT=6881 `
  -p 8080:8080 `
  -p 6881:6881 `
  -p 6881:6881/udp `
  -v ./downloads:/downloads `
  lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest
```

I think I've finally found out where the problem is.

Thanks for all and I'm sorry for all this thread has not to be exactly a podman's bug! ;^)

> Thanks for all and I'm sorry for all this thread has not to be exactly a podman's bug! ;^)

Well uid mapping is part of how rootless podman works. You likely want to add something like `--userns keep-id:uid=1000,gid:1000` to your run command to keep the permissions the same. Or instead use  `-e PUID=0 -e PGID=0`.

And if this is really a permission errors than the QT error is just bad as it doesn't mention that (or even which file it tried to open)

Hi again!

Well, the first command has a typo: "`--userns keep-id:uid=1000,gid=1000`"; changed: "`:`" with: "**`=`**", and it works. But fails.
The second, "**`-e PUID=0 -e PGID=0`**", do really works.
